articles.title: Theoretical Distinctions Between Emotion and Cognition Content in Supportive Messages: Examining Effects and Mediating Mechanisms
articles.abstract: Communication Research, Ahead of Print. themes: Emotion versus cognition content distinctions in supportive communication

articles.core_question: How do emotion and cognition content in supportive messages differentially affect recipients' well-being through distinct mediating mechanisms?

articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study investigates how players construct and articulate emotionally meaningful relationships in otome games. Drawing from 293 open-ended responses from Chinese players, a thematic analysis identified six recurring themes:authentic romance,...
articles.authors: Elena Yifei Zhao, Nicholas D Bowman
themes: Parasocial romance and authentic emotional connection in human-AI/character interactions
Gendered performances of love and reciprocity in digital gameplay
Affect labor and emotional meaning-making through ludic narratives
articles.core_question: How do players of otome games construct, articulate, and experience emotionally meaningful romantic relationships with fictional characters through gameplay narratives?

articles.title: From search engines to AI agents: Interface control and the restructuring of communication power
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Control over digital interfaces has become a significant aspect of geopolitical struggles. This article advances an analytical framework illuminating how global communication power manifests across three key interfaces: search engines, social media, and ...
themes: Power and control dynamics in digital interfaces as sites of geopolitical struggle
The evolution from traditional search engines to AI-driven communication agents and its implications for interface governance
Restructuring of global communication power through platform control and interface design
articles.core_question: How does control over digital interfaces—specifically search engines, social media, and emerging AI agents—restructure global communication power and manifest as a dimension of geopolitical struggle?

articles.title: “Douyin is taming us”: Visibility, governance, and feminist discourses on Douyin
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Drawing on 14 in-depth interviews, this study examines how feminist content creators on Douyin navigate platform visibility, governance, and gendered censorship to disseminate feminist discourse. Findings show that participants position themselves as...
themes: Platform governance and algorithmic visibility as mechanisms of discursive control
Gender and censorship: the gendered policing of feminist speech on social media
Strategic negotiation: how content creators adapt feminist discourse to platform logics
articles.core_question: How do feminist content creators on Douyin navigate platform visibility, governance, and gendered censorship to disseminate feminist discourse?

articles.title: From For You to the family table: Adolescent consumer learning and reverse socialization on TikTok
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study examines how TikTok functions as an agent of consumer socialization and facilitates intergenerational knowledge exchange within families. Drawing on 20 in-depth interviews with adolescents aged 11–16, the study investigates how they acquire, ...
articles.authors: Bo Dhondt, Dieneke Van de Sompel, Liselot Hudders
themes: Consumer socialization theory in digital environments (social media platforms as socialization agents)
Reverse intergenerational knowledge transfer (adolescents influencing family consumer behaviors via TikTok)
Algorithmic culture and platform-mediated learning (the 'For You' page as a pedagogical/curatorial mechanism)
articles.core_question: How do adolescents acquire consumer knowledge through TikTok and subsequently facilitate intergenerational consumer learning within their families?

articles.title: Navigating uncertainty in human-AI relationships: An investigation of communal uncertainty reduction strategies
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study extends uncertainty reduction theory beyond dyadic interaction by introducingcommunal uncertainty reduction strategiesin human-AI socio-emotional communication, wherein users navigate AI-related uncertainty by engaging with both AI chatbots ...
articles.authors: Hongyuan Gan, Han Li, Jinyuan Zhan, Renwen Zhang
themes: Uncertainty reduction theory extension beyond interpersonal dyadic communication
Human-AI relational communication and socio-emotional dynamics
Collective/communal strategies for managing technological uncertainty
articles.core_question: How do users employ communal uncertainty reduction strategies to navigate uncertainty in human-AI socio-emotional relationships?

articles.title: Trending women in STEM: Visual space-making on TikTok
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. While previous research has established foundational knowledge on how women in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math) social media content creators use digital platforms to combat stereotypes, less is known about how they make space for ...
articles.authors: Jiyoun Suk, Sara Holland Levin, Jocelyn Steinke, Kyle Schnitzer, Amanda Coletti, Christine Gilbert, Anne Oeldorf-Hirsch
themes: Digital identity construction and self-representation among underrepresented groups in STEM
Social media as counter-public space for stereotype resistance and community building
Visual rhetoric and vernacular creativity on short-form video platforms for professional identity work
articles.core_question: How do women in STEM use visual practices on TikTok to create space for themselves and challenge stereotypes while negotiating professional and gendered identities?

articles.title: Growing up with misinformation: A mixed-methods study of the perceptions, experiences, and responses among the 5- to 12-year-olds
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This mixed-methods study explores how children aged 5–12 years experience and respond to perceived misinformation on social media. Drawing on mobile ethnographic data and a survey of 401 Danish children, the research shows that children perceive ...
articles.authors: Freja Sørine Adler Berg, Lene Heiselberg, Thomas Enemark Lundtofte, Lena Frischlich
themes: Children's misinformation perception and credibility assessment
Age-related differences in digital media literacy and skepticism
Ethnographic and survey-based mapping of children's lived experiences with online falsehoods

articles.title: Mitigating the News-Finds-Me Perception: Evaluating the intended (and unintended) effects of educational warnings on political learning
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Social media foster a News-Finds-Me perception (NFM), whereby individuals believe important news will “find them” without actively seeking it. Although the negative consequences of NFM are well-documented, strategies to mitigate NFM’s effects remain ...
articles.authors: Eunchae Jang, Mengqi Liao, Chris Skurka, Homero Gil de Zúñiga
themes: News-Finds-Me perception and its democratic consequences
Media effects of educational interventions and corrective messages
Incidental versus intentional political learning in digital environments
articles.core_question: How do educational warnings designed to mitigate the News-Finds-Me perception influence political learning outcomes, and what are both the intended and unintended effects of such interventions?

articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print.
themes: Platformisation and platform politics in news production
Algorithmic curation and diversity attenuation in media ecosystems
Institutional isomorphism and erosion of journalistic distinctiveness across outlet types

articles.abstract: New AI communication course teaches students literacy and ethics thepointweekly

themes: Career outcomes and professional trajectories of communication graduates

# 本周新闻传播学研究前沿风向

**分析时间**: 2026/5/1
**文章总数**: 45

## 一、热点主题排行

1. Emotion versus cognition content distinctions in supportive communication (出现 1 次)
2. Mediating mechanisms linking message content to well-being outcomes (出现 1 次)
3. Theoretical differentiation of supportive message effectiveness (出现 1 次)
4. misinformation correction and debunking effectiveness (出现 1 次)
5. psychological inoculation and self-affirmation in political belief updating (出现 1 次)
6. signal detection theory as applied to political information processing (出现 1 次)
7. Hybrid organizing and institutional logics in organizations pursuing dual/multiple goals (出现 1 次)
8. Symbolic communication and discursive construction of organizational identity (出现 1 次)
9. Resilience enactment through social media as a communicative practice (出现 1 次)
10. Representational networks and networked communication flows in digital spaces (出现 1 次)

## 二、新兴议题

- Emotion versus cognition content distinctions in supportive communication
- Mediating mechanisms linking message content to well-being outcomes
- Theoretical differentiation of supportive message effectiveness
- misinformation correction and debunking effectiveness

- Social media as counter-public space for stereotype resistance and community building

- Ethics education in emerging technologies
- Curriculum innovation for digital communication
- Human-centered communication in institutional contexts
- Narrative and identity construction in organizational messaging
- The role of empathy and relationality in mediated discourse

## 三、方法趋势

- unknown: 15 篇 (33.3%)
- qualitative: 15 篇 (33.3%)
- mixed methods: 9 篇 (20.0%)
- quantitative: 5 篇 (11.1%)
- experimental: 1 篇 (2.2%)

## 四、综合趋势判断

本周期研究明显向"Emotion versus cognition content distinctions in supportive communication"方向倾斜，Emotion versus cognition content distinctions in supportive communication成为核心驱动变量。
